reviews.review_text: Huge sites. Clean and amenities and grounds well maintained. Friendly and helpful staff

Noosa North Shore Beach Campground is the perfect place to switch off and relax. Falling asleep to the sound of waves crashing and waking up to beautiful sunrises never gets old. The beach walks can last an hour or a whole day depending on how much you want to explore.

The wildlife is part of the experience — stepping out of your tent to find a mob of kangaroos lounging around the campsite like they’re waiting for their morning coffee, curlews giving you the usual side-eye as they wander past, and the resident goannas appearing right on 10am for their daily stroll checking if anyone left goodies behind.

The amenities are always clean and blend beautifully into nature. Easy ferry access makes getting there simple, and if you don’t feel like cooking, the tavern is only a short 7-minute drive away.

A beautiful place to escape the noise, slow down, and completely melt into relaxation.
We have been here many times, great acommodation and awesome location. Glamping tents this time, worth every cent!

We didn’t realize there was a $16 ferry fee to enter and another $16 to leave, so keep that in mind when budgeting your trip. Before arriving at the campsite, we also had to buy supplies from a nearby store where everything was at least $5 more expensive than regular supermarket prices.

That said, we had a really peaceful stay. The campsite is close to the beach, the sites are beautiful, and you can have a campfire as long as you bring your own fire pit. We saw lots of birds, reptiles, and kangaroos, especially in the morning. Totally recommended if you want a quiet nature escape.

Easy check in/check out process. We stayed in kangaroo loop and were happy with the site size, we thought it was quite large. Lots of campers but didn’t fee busy. Lots of wildlife around, we saw kangaroos, turkeys, goannas, ducks, etc.
Lugar lindo
Campground is fabulous. Right on the beach. Amenities clean.

There were a few things this time around- broken ice freezer which was super inconvenient, and by the end of the week, only one washing machine working. Perhaps just one of those things, but for busy periods, like Easter - it was annoying
Great spot to switch off and relax

Great camping holiday, we stayed in swags. Amenities and camp kitchen were great.
Perfect location close to the beach, loads of activities for the kids.

Have stayed here a few times and it is such a beautiful place to stay to unwind and do absolutely nothing for a few days!
It would also be more efficient over the busy holiday periods to have another person at reception for check in/check out.

Great location. Booked 5 sites for a big get together, hosts made sure we were all together.
Great amenities.
Definitely be back

Magnifique campement donnant directement sur la plage et remplie de Kangourous à toutes heures! Lors de la shoulder season pas de problèmes de disponibilité des douches et toilettes. Sachez que vous serez tout à fait isolés, c’est le charme de l’endroit bien sûr, mais prévoyez suffisamment de courses pour votre séjour car impossible de faire des courses sur place. Aussi prévoyez le budget ferry, 36 dollars aller-retour en voiture
The best camping spot near Noosa, just steps from the beach, with great facilities. I’d also like to highlight the excellent attitude and helpfulness of Hannah and Solema. I 100% recommend it.

This was the place where we almost had a face to face encounter with a wild kangaroo!

We arrived a bit late which was a shame because it seemed like a place worth spending more time at. It’s a very charming spot and definitely somewhere I’d like to visit again.

It was quiet but the clear sky, fresh air and the beautiful stars at night made the experience truly special.

​I was not informed of the mandatory $62 cruise transport fee until after the payment was processed. I requested a refund within 20 minutes and followed the instructions to email the next day, yet I have not heard back anything after 4days passed.
Very disappointed.

GREAT CAMPSITE RIGHT BY THE BEACH!
We had some wonderful days here. The location is fantastic, situated right next to the beach. While the beach isn’t patrolled and swimming isn’t advised, it is possible. A real highlight was having the kangaroos visit the site during the evening and night—such a cool experience!
The facilities in the Kangaroo Loop are a little on the small side I'd say and could be bigger, but everything is in great condition, very clean, and well-maintained.
One suggestion for improvement: it would be amazing if the shop offered fresh bread or rolls in the morning. Otherwise, it’s a very good campsite and I’d definitely recommend it!
Friendly staff, great amenities, terrific site. Loved everything from the quiet, the beach, the wildlife ( except the mozzies) and wull return.

Parked up, back doors open, just yards from a perfect beach and sea. A simple site where you need to be stocked up before arriving. Big camp kitchen was impressive.  Nothing anywhere near made it great for star gazing.   Really liked it. 👍
Great spot. Toilets and camp kitchen well cleaned. Tourists loved the wildlife. Great access to the beach

Hermoso lugar! Súper bien equipado, con heladera, frizzer, para lavar ropa. Los baños siempre están limpios y el lugar es tranquilo. Perfecto si queres dormir con el sonido de las olas del mar de fondo 🥰
We had a great site in kangaroo loop, we will be going back for sure!!

Great place to camp.
Very tidy amenities!
Close to the beach.
Great place for the kids.

Great camp ground so close to beach.
Nice to be amongst trees in a great sized site. Quiet, polite and friendly neighbours. Basic amenities were prefect and always clean.
Beautiful setting with lots of wildlife I recommend going here for family holiday

Brilliant - our new favourite spot.
Spent a week in our van on Kangaroo Loop, so peaceful, few hundred metres from the beach, and clean amenities. Sites were so spacious too, with lots of grass. Beach is wonderful and quiet with no cars allowed in front of the camp area so it felt really safe with little kids.
Staff were helpful & friendly. We can’t wait to get back for another stay.
Hard spot to park in/reverse out of due to trees and poles, very very close next to fellow campers, no personal space, literally set up a couple of meters next to each other

reception lady is quite rude and just unfriendly. every single interaction ive had with her ive been respectful and honestly quite nice. just think a tad of respect should be on the table and just better customer service skills in general. beautiful campgrounds though.

Such a great place to stay. Have to wait for the ride to go down to get across but definitely worth the wait. The drive on the beach is beautiful and all the facilities at the camp are great. You are able to get a good set up with the amount of room you have. Everyone is so friendly

Noosa North Shore Campground is one of those places that quietly gets everything right.
Other reviews talk about the friendliness, the calm, and how smoothly everything runs—and they’re right. The communication alone sets it apart: clear, timely, and genuinely helpful in a way that immediately lowers your blood pressure before you’ve even arrived. You know where you’re going, what to expect, and that someone on the other end actually cares that your stay goes well.
Once you’re there, it’s exactly what you want from a beachside campground. Space, breeze, birds, and that feeling of being just far enough from everything to actually relax. It’s not flashy. It’s not trying to be a resort. It’s simply well run, well kept, and easy to be in.
You arrive a little tired and leave a little more yourself.
Five stars. For clarity, kindness, and for making the simple things—booking, arriving, staying—feel quietly effortless.

Will never return to this campsite. Some sites have clear boundaries, however many do not, and there are no posts or markers dividing split sites. I stayed in a split site and we were effectively camping on top of another group due to the lack of signage.

Later in the evening, I witnessed the manager telling another group, who were already fully set up, that they were in the wrong spot and needed to move. This site also had no indication of where the campsite boundaries were. After being forced to move, their campervan ended up positioned over the tent pegs of another campsite, creating a clear safety issue.

This situation is completely unacceptable. Either the sites need to be made larger or every site must have clear signage or physical dividers to prevent this from happening

#noosatourism #noosacouncil

I arrived to check in and was told my booking had been cancelled due to a “no-show” for a night that I never booked or paid for. I received no notice of any cancellation or extra night charge until 7:30pm the night before check-in.
Despite showing multiple emails clearly confirming one night only, the receptionist repeatedly dismissed my evidence and spoke to me in a belittling and dismissive manner. When I asked to move past the issue and simply stay for the night I had paid for, I was instead booked for two nights and told I had to pay an additional $50, again being told “that’s what you booked” — which was incorrect.
When I pointed out (again) that my confirmation emails showed only one night, I was told “the system changed it and that’s just what happens”. No accountability, no apology, and no attempt to resolve the issue professionally.
After 30–45 minutes of back-and-forth — which is completely unacceptable for a basic check-in — and only once I became visibly frustrated, I was finally allowed to check in. I had to apologise to other guests waiting behind me, who themselves acknowledged that I was being ignored despite providing clear proof.
No guest should ever be made to feel belittled or blamed for an error they had no control over. I will never stay here again and would not recommend this place to anyone.

#noosatourism

A very well-equipped campsite, with lovely intimate campsites. Close amentities which are well maintained (cleaned daily), a well equipped camp kitchen with large double fridge, freezer, toasters, microwave, kettles and 4 BBQ plates (electric). Helpful reception with basic supplies available including ice. Very short walk to the beach which is unpatrolled. We stayed in two adjacent campsites with three teens and three younger kids and it suited all of them. Ferry across the river to get there is $32 return for a car, $64 return if you have a trailer on; runs continuously and takes about 3 minutes.
Great spot, good amenities. Beach out front is car free so safe and pleasant for kids.

Great camp ground with fairly new amenities. Drinking water, camp kitchen and warm showers. Toilet paper and soap was always stocked. I do not recommend this camp (unpowered site) in the rain. We got caught in a thunderstorm and most of our camp site flooded. You have to bring your own fire pit or rent one. Beach is beautiful and lots of wildlife around.
Amazing what a place. I'm not a camper but my mate was so everything went really well. Just great to be out there.

5 star is based of location and size of the camp grounds.

There is a lot of space for improvements.
Toilets needs more attention as they get constantly blocked.
Camp grounds especially unpowered sites needs to be address urgently drainage to avoid campers sitting in water when raining. This is must!

Ground keeper Lachlan. It was good to see smile on his face. Bravo Lachlan it's all about keeping guests welcomed and help whenever you can. Looks like your growing well into camping business.

Mosquitoes this year are crazy but not something that ground keepers can stop.  Get prepared before you visit site.

Overall it's nice areas and please keep bookings by ( DATES ) do not change to day as regular campers are losing slowly their regular yearly dates bookings

Keep up good work and hope to see improvements in 2024.
Happy New Year!
Good starter camp place, a good ol' clasic campgroud. Can't wait to come back!
Went camping here with friends and had a super fun time! Beautiful beach!
Great service and price. Sorry about the disruption guys.

We were at the non powered tent sites.   Nice flat, good shade.  Just needs taps at the sites but not a long walk to the camp kitchen. Ablutions became grimey so skipped last day shower and came home. Ocean swim is a short walk.   Beach access for 4x4 is also a stones throw.
The best Campground hands down
Love this place. Shared kitchen with toilets and showers. Beach is a stones throw away and is stunning.

Paid 32 for the fairy to take us over to Noosa north shore as we had never been there before, and there is nothing to do besides go four wheel driving or go to the beach.

Unless you are staying at one of the caravan parks there not much else there.
Very tranquil layout, clean, short walk to the surf beach
The weather was perfect and the ocean was clear.

An EMF5-level storm completely destroyed us, but everyone there was incredibly kind and helpful. Huge shoutout to Steve, who saw the future and notified us of the hail and also helped us after the storm as well. Also, thank you to Noosa North Shore Beach Campground for fully refunding every night we were not able to stay. Lastly, thank you to BOM, who recently unnecessarily changed their entire website and ruined literally everything. I am on my way now to write a horrible review.

Wonderful stay at Noosa North Shore! Sites were clean and spacious, amenities were very clean and seemed new. The beach was gorgeous and quiet. We saw lots of kangaroos! I would highly recommend staying here.
Enjoy the camp ground and location. Lovely beach and swimming in the ocean.

My wife and I had a most enjoyable 5 nights stay with our friends on the front. The disability bathroom is one of the best we have experienced. I have a Magic mobility 8 Four wheeldrive wheelchair. I can access the beach safely at any of the access points , however to exit the beach I can only do this at 1 very low cutting. Unfortunatly this cutting as shown in the photos is extremly overgrown and can only be negotiated with the help of my wife. I understand this is a difficult issue to enviromentily overcome, however this issue has been resolved for example at local surf clubs and the leisha track. Please consider.
👨‍🦼‍➡️

Mooie camping waarvandaan je kunt kiezen of je in de luwte van de bomen of dichter bij het strand waar het meer waait, wilt staan. Toiletten en douches worden iedere dag in de ochtend schoon gemaakt. Er is gelegenheid om te koken in de keuken. Kangoeroes zijn aanwezig net als vogels die je brood wel lusten. Prachtig om mee te maken.
Mooi strand, groot en schoon.

Fantastic campsite. We stayed here for two nights, right by the beach, and absolutely loved it. This is by far the most beautiful campsite in terms of location that we’ve stayed at so far, and as the cherry on top, we also got to enjoy the kangaroos wandering around the grounds.

If I were to give one piece of feedback, it would be about the amenities; they could use a bit of a refresh or deep clean.

But overall, an absolutely fantastic campsite!

This campground has fell below average. If you are not camping in very thick patches of sand holes ( big holes with people getting stuck) you are showering in dirty amenities. This place is usually very beautiful and sort of still is. It is extremely peaceful and relaxing but very sad state of all amenities. I hope they pick their game up as it is nice here, but there is no maintenance work being done here. Even tree rubbish is just thrown over the fence into the revegeation area which looks terrible and very untidy. We were so looking forward to our break over here but have been very saddened and disheartened by how run and unclean it is has become, Amenities being the worse. Come on guys we know yous can do alot better please do.

Amenities always dirty, always occupied. Sometimes with long waiting line. At night everything smells like smoke due to the fires people set.
Our spot was too low for the cars height.
The only nice / special thing was seeing kangaroo eating grass on site at night

We have been holidaying at Noosa North Shore for years and love it, it’s right on the beach and we love the drive to Double Island Point.  Just wish they didn’t change the re-booking system so you don’t have to go on the same date each year, makes it difficult during school holidays.  Great place to stay.

Bon accueil,  nous sommes arrives avec 1 jour de retard et ils ont decalle notre réservation d'une journee. Calme, grands espaces, mais pas d'eau sur les sites et le point de ravitaillement général est très mal situé (arbres, branches,  risque élevé de détérioration des toits de camping car)
Always love staying at North shore, natures playground
Big sites, nice and clean, will be back.

Such a beautiful, peaceful & relaxing spot. Great size caravan sites, not on top of each other. Natural surroundings are wonderful - from the sound of the ocean, fresh sea air, abundant bird life, kangaroos chilling on the grassy sites, well kept park - neat & tidy, good amenities (could be GREAT amenities with a few simple additions - more hooks & shelf/ledge added to showers, hooks on walls in general area to keep gear off wet floor & vanity basins), toilets blocks are good, however could use a hose out now & again. We loved our stay at Noosa North Shore Beach Campground - we'll be back & we'll bring some friends.👍🏼🏕️🏖️🌞🌊👏🏼🥰

We didn’t realise at first that you need to catch a ferry to get to Noosa North Shore Beach Campground, but luckily it runs back and forth from early morning until evening, which made everything easier. Once we arrived, the campground was absolutely worth it.

The facilities were clean and well-kept – kitchen, laundry, and bathrooms all tidy (a little sand left in the showers, but that’s totally expected being right by the beach). The location is stunning: peaceful bush sites, spectacular beach access, and incredible views. We enjoyed both sunset and sunrise right on the beach and even spotted a few kangaroos while heading down for sunrise in the morning.

Our powered site was tucked away in the bush, private and relaxing. The whole experience was restful, quiet, and everything we hoped for. No issues or concerns at all.

Highly recommend Noosa North Shore Beach Campground to anyone wanting to escape the crowds, connect with nature, and enjoy simple, relaxing, beachside camping.

Noosa Northshore Campground is pure magic for anyone who loves the wild. Wildlife roams around the campsite, and at night the sky is so clear it feels like you could reach out and touch the stars. The beach is just a few steps away, and for a small $15 fare you can explore across the river  it feels like a mini adventure every time. Perfect for camping, stargazing, and truly connecting with nature.”

I think it is good value for money..
The amenities were always clean while we were there. The campgrounds were clear of rubbish.. They had a wood fire pizza van come in on Friday night.

The only downside for the campsites we had (non powered) was that there was a lot of gravel base on the sites. Probably ok if you have a van or camper trailer but a bit rough for getting our tents setup getting the pegs in the ground..small issue that can be overcome with a good groundsheet.
Lively camping spot

A great place to use as a base for accessing the beaches of the Cooloola National Park or staying local to experience the beach at the park.   It was great to see the whales of a morning playing not far from the shore.
Have been going there over the last 25 years and will be back.
Fantastic place to stay, beach, birds wildlife, the works. Firepits for hire complete the experience. Super clean facilities

Outstanding showers amenities
Cooking facilities very good not the crap electric these are gas !! Brilliant ! Loved the track thru the park to shops a great walk !!
The only thing !!! The dump point a good drive away that’s ok we found it
There’s a lady staying there in a Winnebago drug affected extremely abusive and aggressive toward her dog ! It barked at us she picked it up by the neck and threw it into her van yelling at the top of her voice profanities
Very scary she was talking to herself I told others to stay clear of her she was unpredictable little kids around dog unleashed .. just put a bad vibe out we were glad to leave only because of her !!
My favourite camping site. Beautiful place to see the stars ✨

Prachtige plek aan zee en goede faciliteiten. Helaas staan er mensen op de camping die wel geld hebben voor de ferry, maar niet om zelf boodschappen te doen. Op een avond is voor 60$ aan vlees en andere boodschappen uit de koelkast gestolen en de receptie interesseert het helemaal niets. De camping is uiteraard niet schuldig maar het ontbreekt de camping beheerders aan ieder vorm van empathie. Jammer, gemiste kans.
Beautiful place with kangaroos.
We love Noosa North Shore for its serenity and natural setting, the facilities are always first class. We'll be back again no worries

Really beautiful camping ground, super close to the beach. Was disappointed that we requested to have our camping spots next to each other and were told that this wasn’t possible. Would’ve loved to be up on the kangaroo loop area but when you book you can’t request where you’re booking which is annoying.
Ended up in the bush camping which was lovely, but gravel base - which wasn’t ideal with our crawling baby.
Would give 5 stars if we could choose where we camp rather than be allocated at random.
Great Place to stay over night!

The amenities are very basic – there’s a grill, sink, and fridge, but no sponge, paper towels, or cooktop provided. You're expected to bring your own cleaning supplies and cooking equipment. That said, the location is secluded and peaceful, and you’ll likely have the beach all to yourself – perfect if you're after a quiet getaway.

The location is gorgeous. The campground offers direct access to the beach and is regularly visited by roos.
The facilities were insufficient in number and cleanliness. There are only four showers and a total of six toilets for the large campground.
Plus, you are reliant on a ferry to get to the campground.

Quite and magic campground, you get all the facilities and well organized and clean!
The beach it's just stunning!
Highly recommend!
A great spot especially for large groups and kids!! Facilities are amazing, easy access to beach for excellent 4x4 adventures

Just felt it was too expensive as you have to pay $32.00 each way with your car and caravan to go on the ferry and then pay for your caravan site.  Also reception is 700 metres away from actual park in a different area and very limited food supplies.

What a great little campground. Very clean modern facilities. Well lit. Lovely kiosk (the local Sunshine Coast icecreams are the BEST). Decent amount of vegetation between tent camp sites to offer some privacy and didn’t feel squashed in.

Am amazing location and definitely the highlight for campsite choice whilst travelling from Cairns to Brisbane. The site provided a welcomed break away from the tarmac and the sunsets and sunrise a true gift.  Great facilities and welcoming staff.  Definitely recommend.
Always a treat, away from the hustle and bustle  but a short bus or ferry ride to Hastings St or Tewantin.
It would be nice if you could actually talk to someone

We stayed 3 nights and in this time we would have seen 3 incidents hitting these stupid posts costing at least $6000 in damage. Why aren’t the numbers on the posts on the back or on plates on the ground. I have stayed in hundreds of caravan parks and grounds and never seen posts at the front of a site. Also it is now $60 return on the barge and $60 a night for 2 adults and they don’t even clean the toilets in our area the whole time we were there.
Friendly staff, great ammenities which are well kept. Camp sites have practice layouts and all seem to be close to toilets. Camp kitchen is designed. Brilliant beach access!

Ferry access is no problem, just a bit pricy for a car and caravan over $60 for a return trip.

Friendly, accommodating staff, easy booking process. If you download the park map, you can try and book a specific site.

Unpowered sites are tucked away in the bush, and if you're self contained, these are the pick of the park.

Powered sites are in a grassed, open section, no trees. Easy caravan parking/setup. Not many sites are drive through, if any.

Amenities are clean, contemporary and we'll maintained. Toilet/shower block, large camp kitchen with a microwave, electronic jugs, toasters, sinks, hot water and bbq hot plates. Many undercover tables and benches.

No fuel/portable gas available.
Grey water cannot be disposed of on ground, must use disposal point.
Fires permitted in your own or hired fire pits. Bring your wood. Very limited rubbish disposal points at camp kitchen.
No generators allowed and noise restrictions apply, which is great for such a peaceful spot. No pets.
Bring a bikes to make it quicker to get to amenities. Most tracks suitable.
Whole park is quite flat.

Plenty of wild life, big Roos and Goannas, heaps of birds, especially scrub turkey.

Very relaxing place to stay. Surf, walk, talk and eat.
Well maintained facilities,  clean, lovely camp spot! Great camp kitchen and long long beach to walk and play on.

My family and I have been loyal guests since 2019, but after this weekend, that ends here. The way I was gaslit, dismissed, and treated with complete disregard today is beyond unacceptable and says everything about how little this place actually values its guests.
Back in April, I booked two sites for this weekend in June and was clearly told there would be only one site separating us. That was fine. But when we arrived, we were shocked to find we’d been placed approx four sites apart, with a toilet block in between.
I went straight to reception to try and sort it out, only to be brushed off and told that i had been informed when booking that there were “multiple sites” between us, which was a flat-out lie. My family heard during the phone call and thought there was only 1 site between us too. Apparently, honouring what was promised doesn’t matter here.
For the price we paid, I’d rather stay at the local BIG4 where they actually respect their guests and get the basics right. Never again.

First time staying here since the upgrades, and what a massive improvement! The additional toilet facilities make a huge difference, especially when you're travelling with little ones. The wash-down areas near the beach access are a great addition, and having powered sites now means we can camp with less gear. Allocated sites also really help prevent overcrowding.

A small suggestion: The showers would benefit from having curtains, as the seats are always wet and it's difficult to keep clothes dry otherwise.

Overall – perfect! Really impressed with the changes.

Just spent the past two days at Noosa North Shore Campground and I’m already planning my return. This place is an absolute gem for anyone looking to unwind, reconnect with nature, and enjoy a true beachfront camping experience.

The location is unbeatable—right on the dunes with direct access to the beach. Waking up to the sound of the ocean and watching the sun rise over the waves was nothing short of magic. The sites are well maintained, spacious, and feel tucked away enough to enjoy some privacy while still being part of a friendly camping community.

Amenities were clean and reliable, and the staff were friendly and helpful. Whether you're into fishing, beach walks, or simply relaxing under the stars by the fire, this place delivers. It's also a great base to explore Noosa and the surrounding national parks.

Highly recommend for solo travellers, couples, and families. If you’re looking for peace, nature, and some of the best beach vibes in Queensland, Noosa North Shore is it.

Lovely spot and we had a large private site.  Amenities were substandard .. no mop available to mop the muddy floors in the showers, rubbish left on the floors.  Ran out of toilet paper in the beachfront toilets even though we saw a cleaner there the evening before .. not sure what he did as toilets were still dirty and same piece of rubbish was on the floor for 3 days before it was picked up.

Friendly staff, very clean facilities, generous camp area for each van.
beautiful beach.  Safe on beach as there is no driving in front of campground.

Superb spot, just sublime location, excellent amenities. So quiet and spacious sites suitable for bigger vans and vehicles. Appreciated the lack of obstacles to maneuver around..
Lock your fuel up, had  23L of ULP fuel  siphoned out, despite the container itself being padlocked. Also the low life perpetrator vandalised the side of caravan and window with a knife cut, in area of the theft. Our first caravan outing...

The check-in was already rude. I don’t understand why the staff member had to be so aggressive asking about registered names and rego. She didn’t explain anything about the map or the site either. But when we were waiting in line, we overheard her doing introductions with previous customers, which felt really strange.

After the unpleasant check-in, she pointed us to campsite B11. But when we drove there, someone was already there. We had to do a tricky U-turn on the narrow Banksia Loop and ask for the correct campsite number—no apologies from her either.

Everything else about the place is nice — clean camp kitchen and toilets, hot pressurised water, and it’s close to the beach.

Een geweldige plek om te verblijven. Bij aankomst werden we verwelkomt door  een aantal kangaroos. .Mooie locatie  en genieten  van de view. Goed sanitair.Alleen jammer dat je met een beetje grote camper niet dicht genoeg bij het watertap punt kan komen. Tip: Maak voor de campeerders een langere slang om water te kunnen vullen.
Loved our stay!  The powered bush sites were great.  The amenities are cleaned regularly which is lovely.  Thanks for a great weekend - we will be back for sure.
Great

Noosa North Shore is a lovely spot for a camping getaway! They have all the facilities you need to make your trip that much easier, right on the beach and close to an awesome restaurant for those days you don’t feel like cooking! We even had a pizza truck serve pizzas the Friday evening we arrived, how good!
We will definitely be back.

We have stayed here at different times over the years and love everything it has to offer. The only downside this time was some very noisy unsupervised children on bikes, at least 20, of all ages.
I was nearly knocked over going over to the toilets in the dark! We stayed Friday to Tuesday and Friday and Saturday were pretty ordinary, a large club group set up their games area 3m from our van and the noise was horrendous until it got dark. They could have used the beach. Friday night a group watched football on an outside TV with accompanying shouting and cheering. I also like watching sport but not at full bore in a campsite. Yes they stopped at curfew at 10pm but  it's still pretty inconsiderate.
We totally understand kids and families having fun but if you're looking for peace and quiet I'd try to avoid Fridays and Saturdays. The rest of the time wonderful!
Otherwise a truly wonderful spot, we will return but not on a weekend. 😊

Huge sites some grassy and some sandy with power, no water hookup. Good clean toilets and showers. Barge across $30 one way with a van. ( Ned Kelly was a gentleman.) Walk straight down on to beach, showers to wash off at end of track. Drinking water available at camp kitchen.
Absolutely love the place but staff are not friendly. Receptionist who was literally passive aggressive if you're Asian and didn't like the vibe. But campsite is very astonishing and beautiful.

My partner and I had a lovely weekend here. Arrived late but the process upon arrival was easy. Lots of wildlife and obviously very comfortable kangaroos within the campsite which was incredible for my British partner.
We were going to go and get something to cook dinner on the Saturday, but we got a text that the pizza van would be around at about 6pm, so we didn't. 6:20pm came by and there was no sign of the pizza van, so we ended up going out for dinner as we had no food which was much more expensive then intended and saw other camp guests there for dinner due to the same reason. This was disappointing but I'm sure it would have been amazing (I do believe it did arrive, just later then stated). Would definitely stay here again though and all facilities well looked after and managed. Thank you :)
Great place to say for day trips to Double Island Pt

Solema (on reception) is a complete let down to the company. I have never known someone so rude to work in customer service. Upon arrival she didn’t even look up from her computer screen to acknowledge us. Genuinely made no effort and looked completely miserable the entire time. Just did the absolute bare minimum.
It’s a shame because it is a great camp site, spacious plot and clean, modern facilities.

One of my regular and favourite campsites. Quite modern and well equipped. I always stay at an unpowered campsite (typically 1 or 2 nights per stay). Loverly bush setting (very well treed) close to the beach and surf. Often find kangaroos and scrub turkeys wandering through the campground and lots of bird life. The camp kitchen is well equipped (fridge, microwave, kettles and toasters) but the electric BBQs seem quite underpowered (flat out browning a sausage). Stainless steel bench tops but remember to bring your own cleaning utensils, dishcloths, dishwashing liquid etc.  It gets very busy here during school holiday times. I prefer the quiet times mid week.

We had a pleasant stay here. The beach is the main attraction—it’s absolutely gorgeous and perfect for long walks. The campground is basic, with clean but dated facilities. The sites are spacious, and we appreciated the privacy. No power at most sites, so bring a gas cooker or plan for simple meals. The drive in is an adventure—definitely need a 4WD. The lack of phone reception was a bonus for us, but it might not suit everyone. Bring all your supplies, including mozzie spray and firewood. It’s a great spot for couples who want to relax and enjoy nature without distractions. Just don’t expect luxury—it’s all about simplicity here.
Pretty place. Management is fairly complacent though.
We had a wonderful experience,  the receptionist was really nice, the place give us all what we was looking for, l would highly recommend this campground
Great spot but the sights can be a little on the small side

Wir standen hier mit unserem Van für eine Nacht. Der Platz ist schön in der Natur gelegen. Der direkte Strand ist wunderschön.
Obwohl um uns rum viele andere junge Leute waren, hatten wir eine ruhige und angenehme Nacht.
Die Duschen und die Küche waren leider nicht sauber und wurden nur unzureichend gereinigt.
Kein WLAN!

The park is absolutely beautifully located. There are not many amenities at the main campground, but they are clean und modern. The beach nearby is amazing. We saw kangaroos in the evening and on the next morning. We also ate pizza from a wood fired food truck. We stayed there on a wednesday.
Sehr schöner ruhiger Platz direkt am Meer wären gerne länger dort gewesen.
En djurpark vid beachen där vi bodde kungligt.

Words make it hard to describe how fantastic Noosa North Shore campgrounds are. Fantastic running facilities and management, you can appreciate the hard work that goes on behind the scenes. My family and I will be visiting for many years to come. Cannot speak more highly of the camping experience.
Beautiful place , our campsite was huge and very private , just love it .

Best campground by far! 😊 Stayed at the non-powered site with a few mates and absolutely loved it. Secluded sanctuary with clean, stocked amenities. Wildlife everywhere and beach access metres away. Receptionist/ Administrator at the front desk was an angel! So helpful and kind 🤍
Great facilities, away from it all, great wildlife and lovley beach. Can't wait to come back !
We had a great stay. Will definitely be back. Thank you.

We have camped across Australia, and this is one of the WORST campsites we have stayed at. Not sure if the other reviews are fakes, but I can’t fathom what drives the 4.5 star rating.

Cons (with a powered RV):
- The massive campsite which has 20+ vehicles has 2 toilets, resulting in queues
- The bigger toilet + shower amenities is an absurdly long walk away. And there are only 4 showers for such a huge site
- The toilets and showers are dirty and smelly. The showers had muck all over the floors, and doors don’t even close. Doesn’t look like it’s cleaned often (or at all)
- The amenities are basic and aging. Even worse, most of the plugs in the kitchen don’t work, so it’s impossible to use basics like the kettle / toaster.
- The powered sites are just power - no water is provided at the site. The “short walk to the water” is 200+ feet away.
- We had to wait for 30+ minutes at check in, it appears that there’s only 1 member of staff who was away on rounds, and didn’t pick up when called repeatedly.

We came from an eco campsite further north, and this one had worse facilities, incredibly.

The one positive is that the location is excellent, right on the beach. But frankly, save your sanity and stay somewhere else. You can drive down to enjoy the beach whenever you want.

The owner has clearly acquired a good piece of land, and done the bare minimum of investment to make it a campsite.

I absolutely love this place. I’ve been here twice and will continue to come back. Ideally located on the northern side of the Noosa river and adjacent to the beach. It’s a large campground surrounded by bush land. There are Kangaroos that roam the grounds daily. It’s located about 500 metres from the ‘third cutting’ which gives you 4x4 beach access to Cooloola National Park. Travel about 50km north along the Sand and you reach Double Island Point. This is a must visit and is just amazing. Only bad thing is it’s a $15 ferry ride now each way. Used to be $10.
very busy this time of year, but relaxing as always

Close proximity to the beach with nearby beach walk accesses. No beach views unless you’re in the powered area. Clean amenities. Plenty of wildlife saw kangaroos and a couple of carpet pythons. Close drive to beach 4wd access. Nice kitchenette
Amazing location and heaps of room in each campsite

My recent experience at Noosa North Shore was very disappointing. I had planned a camping trip for my parents, who were visiting from Europe for their first time in Australia. Seven weeks before our trip, I called the reception to book a spot for four people and two cars, as the website was difficult to use. The staff informed me of two available private spots and instructed me to make two separate bookings online, assuring me I could note that we were traveling together.
However, when I called the day before our arrival to confirm, I couldn’t reach anyone. Previously I only received a generic confirmation without details about our spots. Upon arrival, we found that we were assigned to sites at opposite ends of the campground. To add insult to injury, the staff blamed us for not making a group booking through the phone (which I tried but was told otherwise!) or via email, showing no accountability for the mix-up at all.
They did move us closer together, but we ended up in Banksia Grove, which offered little privacy with three or four other campers being right between our spots. This ruined our first night, and despite our attempts to create some privacy the following two nights by squeezing our trailer next to my parents’ camper van on one site, it remained uncomfortable.

In short:
•	Confirm group bookings via phone or email.
•	Don’t expect accountability if issues arise.
•	Avoid Banksia Grove if you want privacy.
•	The online booking system needs significant improvement.

Only giving two stars because of the facilities and the location next to the beach.

Good location but not all sites are equal.

Booked for four people, was given site B1. Couldn’t fit my 4WD and a tent, so definitely didn’t have enough space to move around and cook in etc.

Asked the woman on the reception if we were given the correct site as it was very small.
She was really rude and unhelpful. She said that there is never usually a problem and that all sites are 6m long but vary in width. Said that people with rooftop tents have camped there before but we did not have a rooftop tent so this wasn’t much help.

I asked if we could move to a larger spot but was told everything was booked. I think this is her default response as a few reviews on here have mentioned that. I looked around the site on the day we arrived and left and it definitely wasn’t full.

After a back and forth with the woman somehow another site became available on the powered side of the site. This was about three times the size of B1 and ended up being a great stay. I would come again but would definitely request a larger site before booking. It is a shame the woman on reception was so confrontational.
Lovely campground, close to the beach with good size sites. Facilities are well maintained and staff friendly
Clean facilities (showers, toilets), well maintained tent area,  fantastic location at Lake Cootharaba, gateway to the Everglades. And dog friendly! So nice to see!

Beautiful campground on the ocean.
Great facilities.
Make sure you get in early though if you wish to use the camp kitchen.
Underbar camping precis vis havet, fräscha duschar, toaletter och kök. Kängurur hoppas omkring utanför😍 fantastisk strand!

Just a wonderful get away and so much fun
Very friendly staff - we always look forward to our trip
Great campground next to the beach.
A very nice place to stay. Very relaxing and friendly place. Beautiful  drive along the beach ⛱️ 😍

Title: Disappointing Experience with Staff Member

I recently stayed at Noosa North Shore  Beach Campground and encountered a disappointing experience with one of the staff members. I had specifically requested to book two sites together to be near a friend, but the staff member did not honor this request. Instead of attempting to assist me, they responded with an unfriendly and dismissive attitude when I raised my concern.

I felt disregarded as a customer, and the lack of support made the situation even more frustrating. Campgrounds should prioritize customer service, and unfortunately, this experience left me feeling unwelcome. I hope the management addresses this issue to ensure better service in the future.

Weeks before arrival, Separately we did a booking online with our friends. So I called the office, requesting to put the 2 camping sites together if that's possible.
The lady on the phone was so rejective, saying nothing she can do. When I asked why is that, she said because they are fully booked. When I suggested that cannot swap with someone, she was only repeating that nothing she can do at the moment.
That was so frustrating as our site were very far from each other. My friend call them as well, he got the same lady with the same result.

Next day I thought l might try again. Lucky me I got another lady who was very undersrandig and helpful. She straight away said not a problem can put us together and told me exactly which sites we got.

On arrival at the check-in, unfortunately, we had the same rejective lady and separated us again. When I was telling her that we already got sites together sorted by her colleagues, she said she cannot see that in the system and started repeating her self, that she cannot do anything and was telling us all the confused nonsense reasons.

During our short time in the office we witnessed numerous issues she was creating. A just checked in guest came back that the boomgate fob didn't work. Before checking the fob, she straight away said that because he's not using it properly. Turns out the fob wasn't working.

Another checking in guest front of us was complaining that his days in the booking were messed up.

We could also hear someone on the phone almost shouting at her, with booking days messed up, but only she was repeating again that there was nothing she could do.

She gave us the wrong code for the family room at the amenities as well.

When I told her that I was going to give her a bad review because not just messing things up but doesn't want to help either, she accused me that I'm a bully.

I might be a bully, but she shouldn't be in the hospitality industry with this arrogant, not helpful attitude at all.

That's just additionally. The camping ground sent us a message that if we don't feel like cooking, then there would be pizza from 5pm. So we didn't prepare food for the first night. But the pizza was only from 7:30pm when our toddler kids are already sleeping. So we had to set off to buy some food.

I could go on and on with a bunch of other issues, but probably that's enough for me to give my very first 1star bad review to the place what I'll avoid far away and will advice the same to my friends as well.

Natur Campingplatz mit direkten Meerzugang
Man kann mit oder ohne Strom stehen.
Toiletten sauber .
Großartige Tierwelt direkt am Wohnmobil.
Keine Hunde erlaubt.

Location and facilities are awesome.
Admin did a huge mess with our booking, very unorganized, as changes on booking were requested and getting there nothing had been done.
The area is amazing, but the service was very poor.

We love camping at North Shore Beach Campground. It is maintained well & the customer service is great. The options are good - open grassed area for large vans/families & shaded area for smaller vans, campers & tents.
Lazy days by the beach. Park the van and enjoy.

DO NOT STAY HERE. There is an expensive ferry to get to the campsite, which they neglect to tell you on the phone and their website says nothing about the cost. Some of the worst people in the world work here. Some real nasty bullies will try to take as much money from you as possible. Business must not be going well if they’re trying to rinse their customers for all they can. They have a pathetic attempt at customer service where they are unbelievably rude to you and give you false information.

Great campground with fantastic amenities
Well suited to caravans but we camped and still used the power to charge our electronics.
Heaps of wildlife!
Great location, will definitely come back. Only thing is it needs another amenities block in powered site area

This became my favorite campground on my entire trip along the Queensland coast in July 2024. The ferry ride you have to take to come here is more than worth it. The campground is beautifully located and very well maintained. Spacious pitches close to the beach or between the trees that provide some privacy. As the campground was far from fully booked (which is of course a pity for the owners), it was very quiet  and peaceful and you had plenty of space around you. The amenities were also in greatcondition.The beach and the surrounding area are just amaying! I watched a beautiful sunrise on the beach and had several visits from kangaroos on my “property”.
Long story short: a real paradise. I wish it wasn't so far from Germany. ;-)
Saw kangaroos, goannas, whales, dolphins and many bird species.
One word, magnificent

We had a great time, large spacious sites, so are the group sites too. Clean amenities and loos all over the campgrounds. Fires are allowed now too! We’ve been a few times already and this isn’t the last! Beachfront with amenities.
Nice big camp sites. Good showers, just need a few more hooks for hanging clothes & towels. Also need fans in amenities to dry the wet floor.
Abgeschieden, naturnah, toller Strand, wunderbarer Sternenhimmel

What an awesome camp ground to stay at, highly recommend. Great facilities, clean, quiet, large sites, right on the beach and secluded, yet so close to Noosa. Ferry trip to get access was $30. Power sites, tap available to fill tanks but no hook up points at the sites, grey water must be collected. 👌

Quiet and secluded during the week. The $30 punt one way (with caravan) is worth it especially if you stay a while. There are heaps of different birds. The beach is beautiful to walk and have a dip. Fires permitted.

Highly recommended Campsite. Easy to reach (ferry is no problem).
Clean and good amenities. Right at the beach.

Deze camping is echt bijzonder: het is heel natuurlijk gelegen, met veel wildlife te bewonderen tijdens zonsopgang en ondergang. Let wel op dat de prijs van de ferry recent is verhoogd (per juli 2024) naar 15 dollar enkele reis. Als je vanuit hier heen en weer wil naar Noosa ben je dus 30 dollar per dag extra kwijt. De voorzieningen zijn prima, maar zouden schoner kunnen.
Great campground, good clean amenities and camp kitchen. 5 Stars for that. Not so many stars for the continuous flood of emails and sms's about the booking. STOP!

This was an unexpected high of our trip. The campground has a selection of areas to camp. We chose the beach, although we expected to get a view of the sea and didn’t, the area was spacious, and had good basic amenities. The area was teaming with wildlife (kangaroo and birds). The amenities were in good condition and kept clean, as was the large camp kitchen and shower facilities. The beach is situated on the other side of the dunes, which is literally a couple of minutes away from the camp ground,  which we visited at sunrise. To our amazement, prior to dawn the entrance to the beach had several Kangaroo grazing as we watched the sunrise.  Evenings can be spent around the camp fire enjoying this very relaxing site.
The sight is easily accessed via the ferry, which runs frequently and costs 10$ each way. The roads to the site as accessible via car / van, you don’t need a 4x4. We loved this place and would recommend it as a visit.
Had the most relaxing time  facilities were immaculately clean all the time and our large site just metres from the beach was perfect… will definitely be returning
Close to beach, great facilities. Plenty of space in each camp site.
Awesome experience @ Noosa Northshore! Peaceful, beautiful and very clean. Staff are friendly & helpful. Definitely a new holiday destination. Thank you 😊

Count in the budget 20$AU round trip to take the ferry.

We had taken a site for our roof tent, the location is large, the toilets are clean.

The negative point is: the showers.
If you want to take a hot shower it's a failure, they are beach showers.
Beautiful place. Wonderfull amenities
Loved how close to the beach it is but still sheltered from the elements.
Good spot

The campground ranges from private camp sites nestled amid the vegetation to wide open spaces. The amenities were in good condition and kept clean as was the large camp kitchen. The beach is situated on the other side of the dunes. Evenings were spent around the camp fire. We had a relaxing holiday, although, upon booking our sites, we were not told that maintenance was going to be carried out on the road surfaces within the campground. In spite of the small inconvenience this caused we'd be happy to camp here again.

Great place to stay - right next to a great surf and swimming beach and close to beach access for 4wding (about 200m up the road). Clean amenities and super hot showers. Has a fridge and microwave and gas BBQ's for cooking. Bring your own firepit, or hire one from them. Have stayed here a few times now and have enjoyed it every time.
Lovely beach front campgrounds, ample, clean facilities

Travelling Australia and these campgrounds are by far the best. The location is unbelievable. The facilities are clean and modern. Whoever has designed the grounds should get an award. Also the people who manage it and maintain it to such a high standard. Your belongings feel safe and everyone around respects each other so there are no loud parties etc. It is really the equivalent to Rottnest Island in Perth. Now I know why people book so far in advance!! We will be back. (If we can get another booking that is) beware of the birds and especially the bush turkeys. They love to get into your food!
Such a beautiful place

Amazing camp ground in Nature reserve.  Beachfront location. Clean & tidy, friendly.
Powered sites but no water so tanks in van need to be filled up before arrival. Highly recommend!!

We loved it! We spent 2 nights at Noosa North Shore Beach Campground and we were so sad to leave. The park is immersed in the nature and it feels to be in heaven, amenities clean and a lot of space between sites. 100% recommend
Had a great week. Grounds well looked after. Amenities always clean. Plenty of room on all sites. Definitely will be back.
Lovely campground. So close to the beach, and far from the crowds. Watching kangaroos roaming around was amazing!
We had a great time. Amenities were cleaned and modern.

Location is great! Very quiet and directly by the beach (bring mosquito repellent!). You need to take the FERRY.
However the camp sites (unpowered) are very muddy. Also the camp “kitchen” is just a bbq area. There is no real stove so cooking in your pots and pans won’t work! The showers are really clean and there is warm water. Water pressure however not the best.
Staff is very friendly and late check-ins are no problem.

I had booked two sites for the 19th to 21 April 2024.  Camping in inclement weather is not a strange thing to someone who has lived nearly 60 years within Queensland and has frequented the Noosa and the Noosa North Shore area regularly however, being allocated the lowest camping spaces with non-levelled site's were water poles and floods despite other adjacent am sites in and around the same areas that do not flood, and being specific in our request that we are looking for tent sites and not for a caravan. I find it incredibly Frustrating that we would spend the weekend wading around in water  from other sites flowing onto our site !!!
Is it not reasonable to that the caretakers of this site would understand levels, run-off and inundation and appropriately Insure that there are channels in around these sites to drain the water away .
We have 50 to 75 mm of water around the tent!!!
This is extremely unsatisfactory and will be looking to all sources of social media to post our comments and photos. We will post the photos of our campsite compared to those around us were caravans on sites that are not inundated and flooded by water  The rain events of this weekend and not extraordinary by any means and would not constitute something out of the ordinary
lift your game and provide sites that are proportion to the fees that charge

What a fantastic stay. Clean ablution block, well maintained sites. Wild life in abundance. May I put a request in for some water bowls by the external showers for the birds to drink. I used our Frisbee as a water bowl and they loved it. It would be great to see. We look forward to seeing you again soon and making a fire in our fire-pit.

Beautiful place to camp.  Usually very quiet and respectful place. Loved the school holidays kids play noise this trip, made me laugh out loud - but usually don't stay during these periods to allow families to be there. A bit more shade would be nice but I had shade so not complaining. I appreciate that it is NO DOGS camping. Thank you for that xx  I have dogs at home. They need a break from me too. See you next chance I can get away.

We loved staying at Noosa North Shore Beach Campground.  We had a powered site in a bush setting, giving us shade and privacy from other campers. The amenities were very clean and generous for our needs.  We loved the setting, the staff were helpful, the site was beautiful.  Can't fault it.

Very friendly staff on arrival. They had a lot of rain before we got there so we had to wade through standing water to get to the electrical hookup box. The bathroom/showers have to be used by many people so there are lines and it’s not real clean. Really appreciated the community kitchen area. Beautiful beach. Overall we liked it.

In perfect weather it would be amazing. Good facilities and amenities. Allowed to bring fire pits. We were lucky to have the pizza van there on Friday night and the kids loved it. Unfortunately for us the weather was rubbish in the lead up and the campsites were large muddy puddles torn up by car tires in the entry points to each. It was a serious muddy mess. We ended up squeezing onto our friends van site as they are made up with road base there was no mud. It was definitely crowded.
Lovely campsite right on the beach, really friendly atmosphere for a solo traveller!!! Good facilities and camp kitchen is lovely! Definitely want to go back!

Today top it all. We were told we can't book in before 11 am we arrive at 13. 13 then we were told we can't book in till 13.30 the staff is on break unbelievable. Should U not break ure team time up so someone is at the desk at all times. We paid a lot of money a year ago to get the Easter weekend here surly they can have more than one person with all this extra money they make especially this time of the year where places are this buzzy. Again places getting rid of staff and is understaffed just to take more.now we have been moved from the site we booked a year ago to a much smaller site where we don't fit on with our van and waiting for a complaint from someone. It's 11.04 and still people are parting and music going off not happy
Very small and cramped sites. Main bathroom block was tidy. Very close to beach. Wouldn’t recommend if there is east- south easterly winds.
The best campground - all round! Great amenities, beautiful natural amenity and relaxing vibes :) Most campsites are tucked away in the vegetation, so you also have great privacy!
Great place
Beautiful spot, and so close to the beach. We can't wait to return!
Great location and absolutely wonderful place with good amenities! Well maintained property. Will go back again for sure!

A good little park with lovely hidden sites from heat, rain and wind.
There was not a huge amount to do for our kids. The pool was nice and big.
Fabulous stay in the dog friendly cabin. Everything you could want or need.

This stay helped me realise that I'm not much of a bush camper... There were too many mozzies! I usually don't get bitten but I got smashed!

I would of liked a patch of concrete to step out of the van onto - not a bush camper... Hahah!

They were really helpful with getting the authorisation to get my assistant dog allowed.

I did checkout a day early due to the mozzies but if camping is your thing this place is pretty great! The camps are spread out a bit, the showers were great, reception peeps were friendly and if you have kids the kids looked to be having a blast racing around on their bikes in packs!

I was surprised to get a credit when I checked out early, I've asked they pass it onto someone else as I have zero desire to go back(mozzies).

This was a lovely park and handy for all our needs. The camp kitchen is generous in space, fridges, cooking facilities. Its not to big just perfect. The staff are out cleaning the BBQs and facility's daily.
Totally enjoyed our stay.
Excellent place!!

Underbar vildcamping långt från stan, mitt ute i nationalparken precis jämte stranden. Liten Färja ut och sedan bra asfaltsvägar hela vägen ut till campingen. Fint gemensamt kök, tvätt, toalett och dusch. Och det bästa av allt alla djuren tätt inpå knuten.
Camped at the unpowered side. Beautiful, shady spot amongst the trees. Great camp kitchen and good toilets and showers. Close to the beach and a wonderful spot.

This is no doubt an amazing location and the sites are great!

I rarely give negative feedback but after speaking to staff about my concerns on two occasions. We were disappointed that there was no soap in the mens toilets for over three days and while I understand the dispenser was not functioning another temporary one could of been provided. At one point the Mens Toilets were not functioning, yes they called a plumber but afterwards one toilet was not functioning most of our stay leaving only two In the main block! The urinal in the Mens toilets is broken as are some of the barbecues in the common area. Hair was in the drains in the showers? Add to this toilet paper running out.

We recently stayed at Coolum beach caravan park and the toilets were immaculate!

Hygiene is very important and with Covid-19 it’s essential to be able to keep clean and healthy!
I hope this feedback will result in an improvement at an otherwise amazing place!

I stayed here again two years later and nothing much has changed toilets are not cleaned properly, considering the money they are charging they could surely afford to keep the place clean. Very disappointing!

I can NOT recommend this camping place. I got sick and couldn’t attend my stay on an unpowered site and the staff were really unfriendly!! They also declined to give us our money back (for only one night)

Absolute gem!

Great location, stunning views and clean amenities. Keep up the good work 👍

One negative thing about this place are people who stay here do not respect anything!

We came to make coffee at 5am in the camp kitchen and one of the sinks was just filthy with someone's curry leftovers or whatever that was. Pure filth.

By no means the above is staff's fault, as who knows what time at night the people cooked their food and decided to leave kitchen sink disgusting.

It's shame that people can't clean up after themselves and have no respect for other guests or the staff.

Another thing, the toilets by the beach were all over the place this morning - toilet paper everywhere and toilet wasn't even flushed after someone used it (I'm guessing these are one of many children).

As I said, the place is stunning and amenities are super clean until guests come back.

Would go back in a heartbeat 👍

Outstanding location. We were 30 meters from my the surf. Make sure your tanks are full. No water connections, however water availability is fine for Jerry cans. Factor in ferry costs. We spent $84:00 in two days. Will 💯book again. Its gets extremely busy but is very quite. 🩷

Arrived to simple check in and then Bob directed us into our site. The service is fantastic. The toilet/shower block was spotless evertime I visited and was a pleasure to see this in a well maintained park. The park has more than enough amenities with camp kitchens, pool and playground.
Clean and well maintained facilities next to a beautiful beach. Only downside was some of the camp sites can be quite small and/or poorly marked. But otherwise an amazing place to stay.
lovely park dogs welcome we will be back

Perfect spot
Powered sites
Good amenities walk to the beach ⛱️
Not too big and not too small ,just right ,good value

J’ai été déçu par les partis communes, surtout les salles de bain et l’espace laundry. Ce n’est pas propre !
Le personnel est pourtant sympathique et la vue sur la plage très agréable.
I never spoke with staff from caravan park to confirm that I enjoyed my stay! The park is very cramped, averaged amenities and very over priced. I would not stay there again.

Good camp ground close to beach and entry point to the beach to drive to Double Island Point.
It’s a shame the park didn’t have a recycle point close to the kitchen. The bins provided at the kitchen were complete over filled and not cleared often enough.
Great
A good place, quiet and good quality/price.  Recommendable.  Clean bathrooms and well-maintained common spaces

Its a lovely place to stay with a great outlook. Sadly though a few things need attention.
The light in the southern toilets didn't work, the soap dispenser constantly ran out of hand wash and the showers need a really good clean.
Great ammenities-clean and tidy. Spacious campsites- close to the beach!

A natural camping caravaning place to go. U need the ferry to get there so B all traffic, beautiful beach and bush totally inviting. Amenities clean ,showers hot, a bit of a walk if your site is at the perimeter of the park but gives you time to enjoy the kangaroos, turkeys, goanna's,wildlife.If u love camping  u will enjoy this place,if u want all the mod con's go somewhere else.
Enjoyed our stay. Friendly staff, great facilities, cool layout of the camping areas amongst the trees.

We chose this park because the pool looked fabulous (and was) and Tewantin is a quiet, friendly town. The villa was basic but comfortable, with air conditioning and ceiling fans, a good shower and comfortable bed, along with good cooking facilities. Having no vehicle we relied on the local buses which were right at the front gate, although they finished before 7 each day. Park staff were helpful, responsive and friendly. We would go back.
Excellent facilities good for families and novice campers
Not sure why there is a review here from me. I’ve never stayed there

Amazing camp ground with lots of wildlife and right on the beach. Note you do have to catch a car ferry to get there,only $10 each way.
Unfortunately I had a terrible stay I arrived 30 mins before sunset I booked a site on the beach. It was crazy windy and the site I got was a literal sand patch so i pegged my tent it and it blew away. Luckily the people camping next to me helped and moved me onto there camp site with more solid ground but that meant I couldn’t reach my power box to charge any of my stuff. I did try to call staff to change campground but they aren’t very good at answering there after hour phone. Despite my bad experience it is a lovely place and I would go back just not in a tent on a beach site lol
Away from busy streets, park was quiet, clean and felt safe for kids to be on bikes and scooters.

Really pleasant experience! the camp grounds were clean and spacious, beautiful views, wildlife and beaches, and staff were incredible! Shout out to Mitch and Lachlan for being extremely hospitable and friendly in my unique situation 😊

Little information available on site or when booking, you need to take a ferry ($22 each way) to the campground and also need to take water. This was only conveyed to us the day we were checking in. Suffice to say we didn’t check in. People do need a little more notice for these things when travelling with kids.
Good place to stay with clean restrooms and enough space betreden de sites.
Non powered site 22 was perfect in it's own treed bay, camping as it should be with facilities.
Beautiful, spectacular views

nice quiet and clean park, close to everything, I recommend.
it is pet friendly next time for sure I will bring even my dogs.
Really nice place. Everything was really clean, and the facilities were incredible.
A nice place to stay. Staff were great and the help to reverse into our site made it easy. Good clean amenities and free WI-FI that worked well. Would stay again.

Always a fantastic place to stay.
Friendly staff & Clean amenities.
Good facilities, site size was okay.

Enjoyed our stay, great campground with very clean amenities. Camp Kitchen is a good size with lots of tables . Caravan sites are a generous size. The only negative is the numbered timber poles could be moved back from the front of the road to a better spot in the site, especially when they are opposite other poles across the road. Makes getting in a bit tight. Apart from that definitely recommend this park.

When we first arrived at north shore camping ground it was our first trip there in 10 years, to our dismay our assigned site was not what we expected and we weren’t too happy with how much it had changed.
However, after speaking with the front desk we were given a nice grassy patch with a beautiful beach view that truely captured the magic of this great camping ground.
We were thrilled to discover the spirit of our once loved yearly camping spot had not waned and we fell in love with North Shore all over again.
Some of the extra additions came to be a added surprise including free showers, fire pits allowed, a very handy camp kitchen and a weekly visit from a delicious local wood fire pizza restaurant.
Apart from being school holidays, it was a great place for camping

Rude staff, they stuffed up our booking. We paid $50 too much but they couldn't give us the money back on the spot.
Thank you for your reply, but we still haven't received our refund. Its been 6 months now!

Very clean
Lots of fauna
Thanks for organising the beautiful beach sunrise
Great location! Barge was super easy, clean facilities. Happy with our stay, would recommend this spot!
Feels like home

Stayed on an unpowered site for 2 nights which cost just under $100. The campground was very busy as it was the first weekend of the school holidays. There is one main amenities block for the whole site that had only 4 showers and 4 toilets, over used and dirty during our whole stay as it just couldn’t handle the amount of demand. As your not able to choose your site prior to your stay we found we were a good distance away from toilets, not ideal when there is little to no lighting during the night. During check in, I also found the lady on reception to be pretty rude and condescending- I had to stand for five minutes and wait for her to finish her conversation with a colleague in front of me with no acknowledgement. I was also scolded for temporarily parking in front of reception despite explaining how their car park was completely full. The ferry to the campsite from noosa also costs $10 per crossing so not ideal if you’re going to go to and from the main town. Overall we would not recommend.
I can't fault this place, love it

We enjoyed our stay in September for three nights. The amenities were clean and although we didn’t need to get extra water it was great to know we could access this. We loved being on a larger site with views out to the ocean. Loved the kangaroos visiting and walks along the beach. A great place to relax and recharge.
Cute little place to stay! Super lovely staff & quiet area!
They are impossible to reach by phone despite countless calls

This is a huge Highlight of my road-trip to east coast. Amazing little gem, lots of wildlife, and so peaceful. Saw so many Kangaroos and birds. Amazing beach. Facilities are Great. I would recommend 100% worth taking the ferry to experience this place.

Nice location. Amenities were clean but very far from site which isn’t good if you want clean drinking water. Check in was absolutely horrendous. I waited an hour from lining up outside in the sun until I got served at the desk. During that time she got up & announced she had to have a bathroom break, then came back inside and announced she was going to have a drink break and walks outside and talks to small child who I assume is her son for a few minutes and then after serving a few more people she announces she’s having a meal break.  Just before I was served I heard her say to the person in front of me that he needs to be accountable for his anger issues. I find that comment to be absolutely disgusting as we all waited a hour to check in and he was not the only person who was extremely frustrated at her. Once it was my turn, she was not helpful with any info such as where my site was or any other park information. I honestly didn’t want to deal with her any more than I had to so I walked out after payment & being handed the access card. Do yourself a favour and put her else where in the park or find a new receptionist.
great place to stay
Lovely quiet park. Very clean and helpful & friendly staff. A little far out, but you can walk to the Marina ( about 20 minutes) and get Ferry into Hastings Street. I would stay here again.
Fantastic place to stay

One of the best foreshore campgrounds we have stopped at. Great facilities
We had power but no water to the sites. Plan ahead.

Great place..
it does get busy so hard to book. So just plan ahead and book with plenty of time.. the sites are well positioned. Toilets and hot showers are great and there is an impressive kitchen that can be used.
Another bonus is no cars allowed to drive on the beach out front so very good if you have small kids

Fantastic place for tired mums with energetic children and on a budget. Great combo of both beach and bush. I usually stay in non-powered site and the kids absolutely love it. Fire every night, sea and sand every day, and kangaroos, goannas, and other awesome wildlife to replace the dreaded devices. Hot showers, communal kitchen and fridges just make it that much easier. Love this place!
Nice large sites, clean amenities ☺️
This place just gets better and better - great improvement to facilities.

Good sized amenities but only 3 basins squashed along the back wall. Too dark to use the mirror and hand soap and sharps needle bin reducing the space at each end.
Requires another bank of basins rather than empty space and a free standing cleaners cupboard:(
Camp kitchen excellent and scrupulously clean.
Nice to have international students camping there and sharing their experiences in Australia.

A great campground that we will visit again in the future. However the amenities seem insufficient for the size of the park and Portaloos replacing permanent facilities for an extended period should be looked at. Location is fantastic.
It was good for what we wanted
Great beachfront camping.
Great stay. Quiet, roomy sites. Great Amenities block. The only surprise was the barge. Extra cost, not budgeted for. Must do my research better.
Beautiful spot, plenty of wildlife and of course Stunning beach.

What a gorgeous little hideaway on the beach. Easy access off the barge, it was clean, and tranquil. Hot showers and toilets available, but I do see we will be having to pay for showers in the future. Massive camp kitchen with fridges and loads of cooking room. A must for a weekender or a longer stay.
Staff friendly but unfortunately DO NOT RECOMMEND for caravans as the sites are small and cramped and there is minimal parking space.
Good amenities. Clean.
Fantastic  a day great price and very roomy sites

Beautiful spot!
Open space, plenty of room! Fires allowed and friendly hopping locals. Kids had a great time making a tree cubby, talking to skippy and surfing the uncrowded little waves. Easy access to beach drive up to DI and we spotted heaps of whales close to shore on their trip north.
Big open sites, flushing toilet/showers and drinking water available.
We will be back!

What an awesome getaway! The sites are huge. We thoroughly enjoyed our stay there. One safety observation, when leaving the park to turn right to go towards the beach cutting it is a definite blind spot, you cannot see oncoming traffic on the right. Maybe a mirror would assist with seeing any oncoming traffic. Otherwise we’ll be back for sure.
The room was too small for the money charged
If you want to sleep in a wild area this is the place, just the sound of the ocean and…kangaroo on your garden!
We've been coming to noosa north shore beach camp ground for many years and still love it, but I have to say that it isn't as well looked after since David and Leesa left.
Book the beachfront camp site, it is worth it! We got K7 and the views are stunning. Additionally the amenities are great, clean and modern. The shower had amazing pressure. I would highly recommend!
